news.title: 12th National Congress of the Việt Nam Red Cross to take place in Hà Nội
news.content: The 12th National Congress of the Việt Nam Red Cross Society (VNRC) for the 2026–2031 term will take place in Hà Nội on July 27–28, featuring 500 delegates representing more than 5.6 million Red Cross members, volunteers and youth across the country.

news.title: Hải Phòng hosts 'Drop of Compassion' voluntary blood donation drive
news.content: The 'Drop of Compassion' event was organized by the Hoa Phượng Blood Donation Volunteer Club in collaboration with Việt Tiệp Friendship Hospital, collecting hundreds of blood units to support regional emergency care.

news.title: Humanitarian Month raises nearly VNĐ762 billion
news.content: A month after it was launched, the 2026 Humanitarian Month mobilised nearly VNĐ762 billion (approximately US$29 million), supporting more than 578,800 disadvantaged and vulnerable people nationwide.

news.title: National 'Red Journey' blood donation campaign kicks off in HCM City
news.content: The 14th edition of the national Hành trình Đỏ (Red Journey) blood donation campaign officially commenced in HCM City, alongside the 'Red Drops from the City Named After Uncle Hồ' festival.

news.title: NA leader calls for long-term strategy on voluntary blood donation
news.content: National Assembly Chairman Trần Thanh Mẫn emphasised the need for the development of a long-term strategy for voluntary blood donation while honouring 100 outstanding voluntary blood donors nationwide.

news.title: Vietnam Red Cross Society at 80: From relief boxes to lasting incomes
news.content: Ahead of World Red Cross Day and the 80th anniversary of the VRCS, Humanitarian Month 2026 marks a strategic shift from emergency relief to sustainable livelihood support for vulnerable households.

news.title: Việt Nam Red Cross launches 2026 Humanitarian Month
news.content: The Việt Nam Red Cross Society launched the 2026 Humanitarian Month nationwide to connect agencies, businesses and communities in humanitarian activities, targeting 80,000 units of blood.

news.title: The 2026 Humanitarian Month will be launched on April 29
news.content: The initiative seeks to connect agencies, organisations, businesses and communities in humanitarian activities, supporting vulnerable groups while promoting voluntary blood donation nationwide.

news.title: Nationwide blood donation drive draws strong response across localities
news.content: Localities across Việt Nam launched activities marking National Voluntary Blood Donation Day, drawing thousands of volunteers and collecting thousands of vital blood units for emergency care.

news.title: National Voluntary Blood Donation Day launched in Ho Chi Minh City
news.content: The 2026 launching ceremony for National Voluntary Blood Donation Day was organized in Ho Chi Minh City, encouraging community participation and recognizing regular donors.

news.title: The 2026 Red Spring blood donation festival takes place in Hà Nội
news.content: The 19th Xuân hồng (Red Spring) blood donation festival takes place across Hà Nội venues, jointly organised by NIHBT and Youth Blood Donation Movement with a target of 10,000 blood units.

news.title: Red Sunday blood donation campaign kicks off in Hà Nội
news.content: Thousands of people, mainly youngsters and students, donated blood at the 18th Chủ nhật đỏ (Red Sunday) campaign to help address severe Tết holiday blood shortages.

news.title: Record-breaking blood donors tell their stories
news.content: Inspiring accounts of exemplary blood and platelet donors in Việt Nam who have donated over 100 times, spreading humanitarian values and saving lives across the community.

news.title: Platelet donation demand rises sharply at National Institute of Hematology
news.content: Medical experts highlight the critical need for regular platelet donors, noting that platelet concentrates have a shelf life of only five days and are vital for surgical and cancer patients.

news.title: Regular blood donations ensure sufficient supplies for emergency treatment
news.content: How regular blood donation habits among Vietnamese citizens have transformed emergency medical care and eliminated seasonal blood shortages across major hospitals.
